title: NIST 800-53 Control Validation

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-generated from legacy markdown 'Regulations' line during the per-UC migration. Requires SME review to fill in version, clause, mode, and assurance for NIST 800-53.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 unknown is enforced — Splunk UC-10.12.41: NIST 800-53 Control Validation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-10.12.41' running on Mixed — auth, change, vuln indexes tagged control_id, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Mixed — auth, change, vuln indexes tagged `control_id`
spl:
index=security tag=nist_800_53
| lookup nist_controls control_id OUTPUT family automated
| where automated="true" AND result="fail"
| stats latest(_time) as last_fail by control_id, host
| sort control_id
description: Operationalizes AU, AC, SI, and IR families via automated checks against ingested security telemetry.

grandmaExplanation: Operationalizes AU, AC, SI, and IR families using automated checks against ingested security telemetry — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ES, compliance frameworks.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Mixed — auth, change, vuln indexes tagged `control_id`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map each control to correlation searches; feed failures into GRC; annual assessor exports.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=nist_800_53
| lookup nist_controls control_id OUTPUT family automated
| where automated="true" AND result="fail"
| stats latest(_time) as last_fail by control_id, host
| sort control_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIST 800-53 Control Validation** — Operationalizes AU, AC, SI, and IR families via automated checks against ingested security telemetry.

Documented **Data sources**: Mixed — auth, change, vuln indexes tagged `control_id`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ES, compliance frameworks. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where automated="true" AND result="fail"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by control_id, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (control × system), Trend (fail rate), Table (open findings).
